key_insight: A combinatorial, high-throughput vapor-deposition workflow enables independent tuning of Cs/Pb and Br/Cl composition to identify wide-bandgap perovskites with high photoluminescence (PL) yields.
engineering_relevance: The study provides a scalable screening strategy and a compositional design rule (Cs/Pb = 1.05–1.20) that can accelerate optimization of lead halide perovskites for optoelectronic devices.
method: Sequential physical vapor deposition for combinatorial synthesis
Automated structural, compositional, and optical characterization
Data-driven screening across the quaternary Cs-Pb-Br-Cl space
evidence_signal: The authors screened more than 500 sample regions and found that a high-energy optical transition is a robust predictor of elevated PL yield, with best performance in a narrow Cs/Pb window.
watch_item: The design rule is derived from a specific material system and high-throughput platform, so transferability to other perovskite compositions or device stacks should be validated experimentally.
paper_title: Tuning the optoelectronic properties of wide bandgap perovskites: Data-driven insights from combinatorial synthesis and high-throughput experimentation
